query_text: Plan a 3-day trip for 5 people from Valparaiso to Charlotte from November 5th to November 7th, 2024, with a budget of $3,900. Accommodations should include a private room, and dining options should feature French, Mexican, and Italian cuisines. No specific transportation, events, or attractions are required.

properties.description: Journey through history with one of the most influential voices of the 20th century. Learn more about Billy Graham and his message of God’s love. Through multimedia exhibits, films, and memorabilia, you'll relive the historic moments of his life. Tour the restored Graham family home, shop in Ruth’s Attic, and enjoy lunch in the Dairy Bar. The Billy Graham Library will leave you inspired by this amazing man and his message of hope.
properties.rankingString: #1 of 399 things to do in Charlotte
properties.name: Billy Graham Library

properties.description: The NASCAR Hall of Fame is where legends live on. Where fun lives on. Where the race lives on. Enjoy amazing artifacts, interactive exhibits, simulator rides and special events at the High Octane Theater. Plus, you can grab a bite to eat in the NASCAR Hall of Fame Café or at the Buffalo Wild Wings restaurant. You also can take home a souvenir at the gift shop to help you remember it all.

properties.description:
The reimagined Sullenberger Aviation Museum reopened on June 1, 2024. The new 35,000 square foot main gallery houses two thematic areas: 1) Aviation Society is about the people and companies that make up the aerospace industry in the Carolinas and features oral histories from people who have shaped its business and culture in recent years. 2) Innovation Nation features iconic aircraft that represent significant advances in technology, design, and safetyn starting with Orville and Wilbur Wright. Hands-on activities encourage visitors and students to write their own innovation stories into the history of aviation. 

The centerpiece of the main gallery is the Airbus 320 "Miracle on the Hudson" aircraft that was successfully landed on New York's Hudson River by Captain C.B. "Sully" Sullenberger. About half of the 155 passengers and crew on board were coming home to the Charlotte Region on that fateful day.
properties.rankingString: #9 of 399 things to do in Charlotte
properties.name: Sullenberger Aviation Museum

properties.description: Using history to build community, Levine Museum of the New South offers rotating and traveling exhibitions that explore issues relevant to the history of the greater Charlotte metro area and spark curiosity about our world today. Their exhibitions and public programs welcome all participants to engage deeply with the diversity of our region’s communities through stories of the past that encourage productive dialogue and deepen understanding of critical contemporary issues.

properties.description: Designed by Machado and Silvetti Associates of Boston, the five-story, 145,000-square-foot facility combines inspiring architecture with cutting-edge exhibitions to provide visitors with unparalleled educational and cultural experiences. Located in the heart of Charlotte's burgeoning city center, Mint Museum Uptown is an integral part of the Levine Center for the Arts, a cultural campus that includes the Bechtler Museum of Modern Art, the Harvey B. Gantt Center for African-American Arts and Culture, the Knight Theater, and the Duke Energy Center. Mint Museum Uptown also features a wide range of visitor amenities, including the 240-seat James B. Duke Auditorium, the Lewis Family Gallery, art studios, a restaurant, and a museum shop.

Cabo Fish Taco Baja Seagrill
properties.description: Cabo Fish Taco is serves lunch and dinner, seven days a week. Serving fresh Baja Mexican Cuisine, featuring fresh seafood, chicken and steak. Cabo has a full bar, featuring over 13 original margaritas and a huge tequila list. In door and outdoor seating available. Also, offering a full to go menu.

properties.name: Le Potager de Charlotte
properties.description: « Le Potager de Charlotte » was created by two brothers, sons of an innkeeper. Both brothers are passionate about health, the environment, and animal rights. « At Le Potager de Charlotte, we aim to demonstrate the richness and delicacy of plant based cuisine made from fresh, local, seasonal and organic produce. »

properties.name: Charlotte's Web
properties.description: Enjoy Charlotte’s Web casual, cozy, comfortable dining. Stop by early and have a drink at the bar. Charlottes Web has distinguished itself by serving delicious food, providing friendly accommodating staff and offering reasonable prices. Charlotte’s Web is known as one of the best all around restaurants in Morris County. Come for lunch or dinner and discover for yourself!
